title: alectra fluminensis
intro: Alectra fluminensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family, which is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Alectra fluminensis has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions or decoctions to make teas or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
